trigger: Science paper on AI bot swarms

trigger: Science paper on AI bot swarms

claim: AI bot swarms are uniquely dangerous because they function as coordinated social organisms rather than simple amplifiers

trigger: Science paper on AI bot swarms

claim: AI bot swarms can create synthetic consensus by destroying the independence of voices that democratic deliberation requires

trigger: Science paper on AI bot swarms

claim: AI bot swarms can poison future AI training data through coordinated web content flooding (LLM grooming)

trigger: Science paper on AI bot swarms
quote: social media platforms must move away from the "whack-a-mole" approach they currently use
claim: Social media platforms need to shift from reactive takedowns to continuous monitoring for statistically unlikely coordination

trigger: Science paper on AI bot swarms
quote: we must make it expensive to be a fake person. Policymakers need to incentivize cryptographic attestations and reputation standards to strengthen provenance.

trigger: Science paper on AI bot swarms
quote: we need mandated transparency through free data access for researchers
claim: Legislation should mandate free, privacy-preserving platform data access for vetted researchers to detect bot swarms

trigger: Science paper on AI bot swarms
